behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection + CLI parsing/output. A unit fixture can validate parser logic, but it can miss real `git` rename status formats and thresholds that only appear when operating on an actual repository via subprocess. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is purely git/CLI categorization, with no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, commit or diff it in scope analyzed by the tool, run the CLI, and assert report shows the file only in `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling depends on real git subprocess output and edge-case encoding/format behavior. Mocked diff text can hide crashes caused by actual binary metadata/path handling. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no LLM/model-dependent correctness and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify at least one binary file (e.g., image bytes), run the CLI against changes including that file, assert process exits successfully and report count includes binary-file changes.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure typically occurs at real git invocation on unborn HEAD (`git` error semantics), which is subprocess behavior. In-process mocks can miss exact exit code/stderr handling paths. Rejected higher levels: behavior is repository state/error handling, not model output or agent chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and successful (or specified non-error) exit behavior.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history semantics across multiple change types. The real failure boundary is CLI + subprocess + repository state evolution; synthetic fixtures can diverge from true git history behavior. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML quality dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across several commits, run the CLI for the target range, and assert reported category counts exactly equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Requires real git index vs working tree semantics and CLI interaction with actual repository state. Mocking can hide discrepancies in how staged/unstaged diffs are produced by git commands. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is deterministic git-state parity, not model-driven.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create identical content changes in two setups (one staged, one unstaged), run CLI in each mode/scope, and assert normalized structured outputs are identical.
